page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-192 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4n family. It features 192 dedicated virtual CPUs and 1488.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 7.75 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated graphics processing units, relying entirely on network-attached storage solutions for persistent data.

The high memory density of this configuration makes it suitable for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets without the need for local NVMe drives. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the allocation of dedicated vCPUs alongside a substantial memory footprint provides a balanced resource profile for enterprise applications, avoiding the overhead of unneeded local storage or GPU hardware.

This server type is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Appropriate use cases include large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and data analytics pipelines that process substantial datasets directly in system memory.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-192 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the c4n family, configured with 192 dedicated virtual CPUs and 1488.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.75 GB per vCPU, designed to support memory-intensive applications. The instance does not include local storage or graphics processing units, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options. Qualitatively, the server offers a dense allocation of compute and memory resources, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that do not require local NVMe drives or hardware acceleration. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data processing, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-heavy analytical workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-192 is an x86_64 server featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and 1488 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-192 features 192 dedicated vCPUs and 1488 GB of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity x86_64 compute instance offering one hundred ninety-two dedicated virtual CPUs and nearly one point five terabytes of memory.
bullet_points: High memory-to-core ratio of 7.75 GB per vCPU
Zero local storage and no integrated GPUs
Optimized for large-scale databases and in-memory processing
